title: Expected Value and Variance for Continuous Variables

epigraph.text: A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over.
epigraph.quote: The expected value is the center of gravity of the probability distribution.

content.text:
The concepts of expected value and variance extend naturally to 
continuous random variables—sums simply become integrals.

content.title: Expected Value (Continuous)
content.content:
The **expected value** of a 
continuous random variable $X$ with PDF $f(x)$ is:
\[
 \E[X] = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} x \cdot f(x) \, dx
\]

content.title: Expected Value of Uniform
Expected Value of $g(X)$
content.content:
If $X$ is continuous with PDF $f(x)$ and $g$ is any function:
\[
 \E[g(X)] = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} g(x) \cdot f(x) \, dx
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Variance (Continuous)
content.content:
The **variance** of $X$ is:
\[
 \Var(X) = \E[(X - \mu)^2] = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} (x - \mu)^2 f(x) \, dx
\]
Equivalently:
\[
 \Var(X) = \E[X^2] - (\E[X])^2
\]

content.title: Variance of Uniform
Properties of Expectation and Variance
content.content:
For constants $a$, $b$ and random variables $X$, $Y$:

1. $\E[aX + b] = a\E[X] + b$

2. $\E[X + Y] = \E[X] + \E[Y]$ (always)

3. $\Var(aX + b) = a^2 \Var(X)$

4. $\Var(X + Y) = \Var(X) + \Var(Y)$ (if $X$, $Y$ independent)
content.label: Theorem
